docs.title: ToyML: Machine Learning from Scratch
docs.text: There are machine learning algorithms implemented from scratch. Let's learn machine learning with simple toy code.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: pip install toyml

docs.title: Links
docs.text:
• Documentation: https://ai-glimpse.github.io/toyml/
  • PyPi: https://pypi.org/project/toyml/
  • Changelog: https://ai-glimpse.github.io/toyml/CHANGELOG/

docs.title: RoadMap
docs.text:
• Clustering: DBSCAN, Hierarchical(Agnes&Diana), Kmeans
  • Classification: KNN
  • Ensemble: Boosting(AdaBoost)
  • Classification: NaiveBayes, DecisionTree, SVM
  • Association Analysis: Apriori
  • Ensemble: GBDT

docs.title: toyml.classification.knn.KNeighborsClassifier
docs.text:
KNeighborsClassifier(
    dataset: DataSet,
    labels: Labels,
    k: int,
    dist=euclidean_distance,
    std=True,
)

The implementation of K-Nearest Neighbors.

Ref: 1. Li Hang 2. Tan 3. Zhou 4. Murphy 5. Harrington

Source code in toyml/classification/knn.py 
def __init__(
    self,
    dataset: DataSet,
    labels: Labels,
    k: int,
    dist=euclidean_distance,
    std=True,
) -> None:
    if not isinstance(dataset, list):
        raise TypeError(f"invalid type in {type(dataset)} for the 'dataset' argument")
    self._dataset = dataset
    self._labels = labels
    self._k = k
    self._dist = dist
    # for standardization
    self._is_std = std
    self._means = vectors_mean(dataset)
    self._stds = vectors_std(dataset)

docs.title: toyml.clustering.agnes.Agnes
docs.text:
Agnes(dataset: DataSet, k: int)

Agglomerate clustering(nesting) algorithm.(Bottom-up)

REF: 1. Zhou Zhihua 2. Tan

PARAMETER DESCRIPTION dataset

the set of data points for clustering

TYPE: DataSet

k

the number of clusters, specified by user

TYPE: int

Source code in toyml/clustering/agnes.py 
def __init__(self, dataset: DataSet, k: int) -> None:
    """Initialize the Agglomerate clustering algorithm.

    Args:
        dataset:  the set of data points for clustering
        k: the number of clusters, specified by user
    """
    self._dataset = dataset
    self._k = k
    self._n = len(dataset)
    self._clusters = [[i] for i in range(self._n)]
    self._dist_mat = [[0.0 for _ in range(self._n)] for _ in range(self._n)]

docs.title: fit
docs.text:
fit() -> Clusters

Get every operation togather, train our model and get k clusters

Source code in toyml/clustering/agnes.py 
def fit(self) -> Clusters:
    """
    Get every operation togather, train our model and get k clusters
    """
    self._gen_init_dist_matrix()
    while len(self._clusters) > self._k:
        # print(self._clusters)
        i, j = self._get_closest_clusters()
        # combine cluster_i and cluster_j to new cluster_i
        self._clusters[i] += self._clusters[j]
        # remove jth cluster
        self._clusters.pop(j)
        # update distance matrix
        # remove jth raw in dist matrix
        self._dist_mat.pop(j)
        # remove jth column
        for raw in self._dist_mat:
            raw.pop(j)
        # calc new dist
        for j in range(len(self._clusters)):
            self._dist_mat[i][j] = self._get_dist(self._clusters[i], self._clusters[j])
            self._dist_mat[j][i] = self._dist_mat[i][j]

    return self._clusters

docs.title: predict
docs.text:
predict(point: Vector) -> int

Predict the label of the new sample point

Source code in toyml/clustering/agnes.py 
def predict(self, point: Vector) -> int:
    """
    Predict the label of the new sample point
    """
    min_dist = math.inf
    label = -1
    for cluster_label, cluster in enumerate(self._clusters):
        dist = min([euclidean_distance(point, self._dataset[i]) for i in cluster])
        if dist < min_dist:
            min_dist = dist
            label = cluster_label
    return label

docs.title: print_cluster
docs.text:
print_cluster() -> None

Show our k clusters.

Source code in toyml/clustering/agnes.py 
def print_cluster(self) -> None:
    """
    Show our k clusters.
    """
    for i in range(self._k):
        print(f"Cluster[{i}]: {self._clusters[i]}")

docs.title: print_labels
docs.text:
print_labels() -> None

Show our samples' labels.

Source code in toyml/clustering/agnes.py 
def print_labels(self) -> None:
    """
    Show our samples' labels.
    """
    y_pred = [0] * self._n
    for cluster_label, cluster in enumerate(self._clusters):
        for sample_index in cluster:
            y_pred[sample_index] = cluster_label
    print("Sample labels: ", y_pred)

docs.title: toyml.clustering.dbscan.DbScan
docs.text:
DbScan(dataset: DataSet, eps: float, min_pts: int = 3)

dbscan algorithm.

Ref: 1. Zhou 2. Han 3. Kassambara 4. Wikipedia

Source code in toyml/clustering/dbscan.py 
def __init__(self, dataset: DataSet, eps: float, min_pts: int = 3) -> None:
    self._dataset = dataset
    self._n = len(self._dataset)
    # distance matrix
    self._dist_matrix = distance_matrix(self._dataset)
    self._eps = eps
    # we do not include the point i itself as a neighbor
    # as the algorithm does, so we minus one here to convert
    self._min_pts = min_pts - 1
    self._coreObjects: List[int] = []
    self._noises: List[int] = []
    # the number of clusters
    self._k = 0
    self._clusters: Clusters = []

docs.title: toyml.clustering.kmeans.Kmeans dataclass
docs.text:
Kmeans(
    k: int,
    max_iter: int = 500,
    centroids_init_method: Literal[
        "random", "kmeans++"
    ] = "random",
    clusters_: Optional[list[list[int]]] = None,
    centroids_: Optional[list[list[float]]] = None,
)

K-means algorithm(with Kmeans++ initialization as option).

Examples:

>>> from toyml.clustering import Kmeans
>>> dataset = [[1, 0], [1, 1], [1, 2], [10, 0], [10, 1], [10, 2]]
>>> kmeans = Kmeans(k=2).fit(dataset)
>>> kmeans.clusters_
[[3, 4, 5], [0, 1, 2]]
>>> kmeans.centroids_
[[10.0, 1.0], [1.0, 1.0]]
>>> kmeans.predict([0, 1])
1
References
  1. Zhou Zhihua
  2. Murphy
Note

Here we just implement the naive K-means algorithm.

See Also
  • Bisecting K-means algorithm: toyml.clustering.kmeans.bisecting

The number of clusters, specified by user.

docs.title: max_iter class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:

The number of iterations the algorithm will run for if it does not converge before that.

docs.title: centroids_init_method class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
centroids_init_method: Literal[
    "random", "kmeans++"
] = "random"

The method to initialize the centroids.

docs.title: clusters_ class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
clusters_: Optional[list[list[int]]] = None

The clusters of the dataset.

docs.title: centroids_ class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
centroids_: Optional[list[list[float]]] = None

The centroids of the clusters.

docs.title: get_initial_centroids
docs.text:
get_initial_centroids(
    dataset: list[list[float]],
) -> list[list[float]]

get initial centroids by a simple random selection

Source code in toyml/clustering/kmeans.py 
def get_initial_centroids(self, dataset: list[list[float]]) -> list[list[float]]:
    """
    get initial centroids by a simple random selection
    """
    if self.centroids_init_method == "random":
        return self._get_initial_centroids_random(dataset)
    elif self.centroids_init_method == "kmeans++":
        return self._get_initial_centroids_kmeans_plus(dataset)
    else:
        raise ValueError(f"Invalid centroids initialization method: {self.centroids_init_method}")

docs.title: fit
docs.text:
fit(dataset: list[list[float]]) -> 'Kmeans'
PARAMETER DESCRIPTION dataset

the set of data points for clustering

TYPE: list[list[float]]

Returns:

Source code in toyml/clustering/kmeans.py 
def fit(self, dataset: list[list[float]]) -> "Kmeans":
    """
    Args:
        dataset: the set of data points for clustering

    Returns:

    """
    centroids = self.get_initial_centroids(dataset)
    clusters = []
    for _ in range(self.max_iter):
        clusters = self._get_clusters(dataset, centroids)
        prev_centroids = centroids
        centroids = self._get_centroids(dataset, clusters)
        # If no centroids change, the algorithm is convergent
        # TODO: better convergence criteria
        if prev_centroids == centroids:
            print("Training Converged")
            break

    self.clusters_ = clusters
    self.centroids_ = centroids
    return self

docs.title: predict
docs.text:
predict(point: list[float]) -> int

Predict the label of the point.

PARAMETER DESCRIPTION point

The data point to predict.

TYPE: list[float]

RETURNS DESCRIPTION int

The label of the point.

Source code in toyml/clustering/kmeans.py 
def predict(self, point: list[float]) -> int:
    """
    Predict the label of the point.

    Args:
        point: The data point to predict.

    Returns:
        The label of the point.

    """
    if self.centroids_ is None:
        raise ValueError("The model is not fitted yet")
    return self._get_centroid_label(point, self.centroids_)

docs.title: toyml.clustering.bisect_kmeans.BisectingKmeans
docs.text:
BisectingKmeans(dataset: DataSet, k: int)

Bisecting K-means algorithm. Belong to Divisive hierarchical clustering (DIANA) algorithm.(top-down)

REF: 1. Harrington 2. Tan

PARAMETER DESCRIPTION dataset

the set of data points for clustering

TYPE: DataSet

k

he number of clusters, specified by user

TYPE: int

Source code in toyml/clustering/bisect_kmeans.py 
def __init__(self, dataset: DataSet, k: int) -> None:
    """Initialize the Bisecting K-means algorithm.

    Args:
        dataset: the set of data points for clustering
        k: he number of clusters, specified by user
    """
    self._dataset = dataset
    self._k = k
    self._n = len(dataset)
    # top level: only one cluster
    # remember that our cluster only contains the sample indexes
    self._clusters = [list(range(self._n))]

docs.title: print_cluster
docs.text:
print_cluster() -> None

Show our k clusters.

Source code in toyml/clustering/bisect_kmeans.py 
def print_cluster(self) -> None:
    """
    Show our k clusters.
    """
    for i in range(self._k):
        print(f"Cluster[{i}]: {self._clusters[i]}")

docs.title: print_labels
docs.text:
print_labels() -> None

Show our samples' labels.

Source code in toyml/clustering/bisect_kmeans.py 
def print_labels(self) -> None:
    """
    Show our samples' labels.
    """
    y_pred = [0] * self._n
    for cluster_label, cluster in enumerate(self._clusters):
        for sample_index in cluster:
            y_pred[sample_index] = cluster_label
    print("Sample labels: ", y_pred)

docs.title: SPOT
docs.text:
SPOT1 algorithm is backed by EVT.

  1. A. Siffer, P.-A. Fouque, A. Termier, and C. Largouet, “Anomaly detection in streams with extreme value theory,” in Proceedings of the 23rd ACM SIGKDD international conference on knowledge discovery and data mining, 2017, pp. 1067–1075. ↩
